seoTitle: How to Use Jira Roadmaps for Project Planning
metaDescription: Learn how to use Jira Roadmaps to add epics, build timelines, manage dependencies, and track progress across Agile projects.
intro: Keeping your team aligned on priorities gets much easier with a visual timeline everyone can reference. This guide covers using Jira Roadmaps to add epics, build out a project timeline, and track dependencies and progress as work moves forward.

steps.heading: Open Jira Roadmaps
steps.body: Navigate to the Roadmaps feature within your project to access the timeline planning view.

steps.heading: Create your roadmap
steps.body: Set up a new roadmap for your project, giving it the scope and timeframe that matches your planning needs.

steps.heading: Add epics and timeline items
steps.body: Add epics and other timeline items to the roadmap, giving each one a start and end date to represent when the work should happen.

steps.heading: Track progress and dependencies
steps.body: Monitor how items are progressing against their timeline, and manage dependencies between epics as priorities shift.

faq.question: How do I use Jira Roadmaps?
faq.answer: Open the Roadmaps feature in your project, create a roadmap, add epics and timeline items, and set dates for each piece of work.

faq.question: Can I show dependencies on a Jira Roadmap?
faq.answer: Yes, roadmaps support managing dependencies between epics so you can see what work is blocking or waiting on other items.

faq.question: Who uses Jira Roadmaps most often?
faq.answer: Product owners, project managers, and Scrum Masters commonly use roadmaps to plan releases and communicate milestones to stakeholders.

faq.question: Can I adjust a roadmap's schedule after creating it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust dates and reschedule items on the roadmap as priorities or timelines change.
